votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking for sortedness, absence of duplicates, and set equality of elements.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking for sortedness, lack of duplicates, and set equality of elements.

The Python functions correctly implement the logic of the Coq predicates: is_sorted checks for non-decreasing order, no_duplicates checks for uniqueness, and all_elements_from checks for set equality of elements.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs: is_sorted checks adjacent pairs which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists; no_duplicates uses set comparison which matches NoDup semantics; all_elements_from checks set equality which matches the bidirectional In membership in Coq. unique_spec correctly combines all three conditions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs: is_sorted checks adjacent pairs which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists; no_duplicates uses set comparison which matches NoDup semantics; all_elements_from checks set equality which matches the bidirectional In membership in Coq. All three conditions combined in unique_spec match the Coq conjunction.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec for concrete inputs: is_sorted checks adjacent pairs which is equivalent to the Coq definition for sorted lists; no_duplicates uses set comparison which matches NoDup semantics; all_elements_from checks set equality which matches the bidirectional In predicate. All three conditions combined in unique_spec match the Coq conjunction.

Adjacent nondecreasing check implies the Coq pairwise order by transitivity; NoDup matches set-length equality; set equality matches membership equivalence. Thus unique_spec agrees on lists of naturals.
Python checks adjacent nondecreasing order, no duplicates via set size, and set equality of elements; these match the Coq definitions of is_sorted, NoDup, and all_elements_from for lists of naturals.
Python checks nondecreasing order (adjacent pairs), no duplicates, and set equality of elements, which matches the Coq is_sorted, NoDup, and membership equivalence for lists of naturals.
